questions.text: Why is this Surah titled 'An-Nisa' (The Women)?
questions.answer: It deals extensively with the rights of women, including marriage, inheritance, legal protections, and their roles in the family and society.

questions.text: What is the primary theme of the first Ayah of Surah An-Nisa?
questions.answer: Human equality and common origin, emphasizing that all mankind was created from a single soul (Adam) and its mate (Hawa).

questions.text: What does the Surah command regarding the treatment of orphans?
questions.answer: Believers are commanded to give orphans their property, not to exchange their good things for bad ones, and not to consume the orphans' wealth with their own.

questions.text: Under what specific condition does the Quran allow a man to marry up to four wives?
questions.answer: Only if he can treat them with absolute justice; if he fears he cannot be just, he must marry only one.

questions.text: What is the 'Mahr' (dowry) and how should it be given to women?
questions.answer: It is a gift given to women upon marriage; it should be given graciously as an obligation, and it belongs to the woman alone.

questions.text: At what stage should orphans be given control of their property?
questions.answer: When they reach the age of marriage and show sound judgment; their property should be handed over to them in the presence of witnesses.

questions.text: What is the warning for those who consume the wealth of orphans unjustly?
questions.answer: They are only consuming fire into their bellies and will burn in a blazing Fire.

questions.text: Who established the laws of inheritance mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Allah Himself has ordained these shares as a fixed obligation; they are not left to human choice.

questions.text: What is the inheritance share for a mother if her deceased child has children?
questions.answer: She receives one-sixth of the estate.

questions.text: What is the 'Nisa' inheritance share for a daughter compared to a son?
questions.answer: The male generally receives a share equal to that of two females, as the male is traditionally responsible for the financial maintenance of the family.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the punishment for those who disobey His limits regarding inheritance?
questions.answer: They will be admitted to a Fire to abide therein forever, and they will have a humiliating punishment.

questions.text: What is the requirement for accepting repentance from someone who does evil?
questions.answer: Repentance is accepted from those who do evil in ignorance and then repent soon after; Allah will turn to them in forgiveness.

questions.text: Is repentance accepted from those who continue to do evil until death reaches them?
questions.answer: No, repentance is not accepted from those who die while they are disbelievers or those who only repent at the moment of death.

questions.text: Are men allowed to inherit women against their will or treat them harshly to take back part of their dowry?
questions.answer: No, it is forbidden to inherit women by force or to constrain them to take away part of what was given to them, unless they commit a clear immorality.

questions.text: How should husbands live with their wives even if they dislike something about them?
questions.answer: They should live with them in kindness; for it may be that they dislike a thing and Allah brings through it a great deal of good.

questions.text: What is the rule about marrying women whom your fathers married?
questions.answer: It is strictly forbidden, as it is an immorality and a hateful practice.

questions.text: How does the Surah define the 'Mahram' (prohibited degrees) for marriage?
questions.answer: It lists specific relatives one cannot marry, including mothers, daughters, sisters, aunts, and those related through foster-suckling (Rada'ah).

questions.text: Is it permissible to marry two sisters at the same time?
questions.answer: No, the Surah prohibits having two sisters as wives simultaneously.

questions.text: What is the rule regarding marrying women who are already married?
questions.answer: It is forbidden to marry women who are already in a marriage, except for those whom your right hands possess (in the context of historical warfare).

questions.text: What is the requirement for marrying women from among the believers or People of the Book?
questions.answer: The marriage must be for the purpose of chaste union, not for secret lovers or temporary lust, and the dowry (Mahr) must be paid.

questions.text: What does Allah desire to do through the laws He has revealed in this Surah?
questions.answer: He desires to make things clear to you, guide you to the good ways of those before you, and accept your repentance.

questions.text: What is the prohibition regarding consuming property?
questions.answer: Believers are told not to consume one another's wealth unjustly, but only through trade by mutual consent.

questions.text: If a believer avoids the major sins (Kaba'ir), what does Allah promise?
questions.answer: Allah will remove their minor misdeeds and admit them to a noble entrance (Paradise).

questions.text: Should believers envy the favors Allah has given to some over others?
questions.answer: No, they should ask Allah for His bounty instead of envying others for what Allah has uniquely given them.

questions.text: What is the 'Qawwamun' role of men mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: It describes men as protectors and maintainers of women, based on the physical and financial responsibilities Allah has assigned to them.

questions.text: What is the suggested first step if there is a serious rift between a husband and wife?
questions.answer: Appoint an arbitrator from his family and an arbitrator from her family; if they desire reconciliation, Allah will bring it about between them.

questions.text: Apart from parents, who else are believers commanded to be kind to in the 'Ayah of Rights'?
questions.answer: Relatives, orphans, the needy, the near neighbor, the farther neighbor, the companion by your side, the traveler, and those your right hands possess.

questions.text: How does Allah describe those who are stingy and encourage others to be stingy?
questions.answer: They are ungrateful for Allah's bounty, and He has prepared for them a humiliating punishment.

questions.text: What is the consequence of spending wealth only to be seen by people (Riya)?
questions.answer: It is the way of those who do not believe in Allah or the Last Day, and their companion is Shaitan, an evil companion.

questions.text: Will Allah do even an atom's weight of injustice to anyone?
questions.answer: No, Allah does not do injustice even as much as an atom's weight; if there is a good deed, He multiplies it.

questions.text: What will be the state of the disbelievers when they see the Prophet testifying against them on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will wish that the earth were leveled over them, and they will not be able to conceal a single fact from Allah.

questions.text: What is the rule regarding prayer (Salat) while in a state of intoxication?
questions.answer: Believers are commanded not to approach prayer while intoxicated, until they know what they are saying.

questions.text: What should a believer do for purification if no water is available for Wudu or Ghusl?
questions.answer: Perform Tayammum: find clean earth and wipe their faces and hands with it.

questions.text: Which sin will Allah never forgive if a person dies without repenting from it?
questions.answer: Shirk (associating partners with Allah).

questions.text: What is the command given regarding trusts (Amanat)?
questions.answer: Allah commands you to render trusts to whom they are due.

questions.text: How should believers judge between people?
questions.answer: They must judge with justice.

questions.text: Who are the 'Ulul-Amr' mentioned regarding obedience?
questions.answer: They are those in authority among the believers (scholarly or political leaders), whose obedience is required as long as it aligns with Allah and His Messenger.

questions.text: What is the ultimate reference for believers if they disagree on a matter?
questions.answer: They should refer it back to Allah (the Quran) and the Messenger (the Sunnah).

questions.text: How do the hypocrites react when called to the judgment of Allah and His Messenger?
questions.answer: They turn away in aversion and seek judgment from false deities (Taghut) instead.

questions.text: Is someone a true believer if they do not accept the Prophet's judgment?
questions.answer: No, Allah swears by Himself that they will not believe until they make the Prophet judge in what they dispute and find no resistance in their souls to his decision.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who obey Allah and the Messenger?
questions.answer: They will be with those upon whom Allah has bestowed favor: the prophets, the steadfast affirmers of truth, the martyrs, and the righteous.

questions.text: What is the attitude of the hypocrites towards going out for Jihad?
questions.answer: They linger behind; if a disaster strikes the believers, they say it was a favor they weren't there, but if a bounty comes, they regret not being with them.

questions.text: Why are believers commanded to fight in the cause of Allah?
questions.answer: To protect the oppressed men, women, and children who pray for a deliverer from the hands of oppressors.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe the life of this world compared to the Hereafter?
questions.answer: The enjoyment of this world is little, and the Hereafter is better for him who fears Allah.

questions.text: What should a believer do when they hear an evil rumor or news about security or fear?
questions.answer: They should refer it to the Messenger and to those in authority among them who can investigate it, rather than spreading it immediately.

questions.text: How should a believer respond to a greeting (Salam)?
questions.answer: They should respond with a better greeting or at least return the same one.

questions.text: What is the punishment for a believer who kills another believer intentionally?
questions.answer: His recompense is Hell, to abide therein forever; Allah is angry with him, has cursed him, and prepared for him a great punishment.

questions.text: What is the ruling for accidentally killing a believer?
questions.answer: The killer must free a believing slave and pay compensation (Diyah) to the victim's family, unless they remit it as charity.

questions.text: What does the Surah say about those who were oppressed but did not migrate (Hijrah) despite having the means?
questions.answer: The angels will ask them, 'Was not the earth of Allah spacious enough for you to emigrate therein?' Their refuge is Hell, unless they were truly weak and helpless.

questions.text: What is the reward for someone who leaves their home as an emigrant (Muhajir) to Allah and His Messenger and then death overtakes them?
questions.answer: Their reward has already become incumbent upon Allah.

questions.text: Is it permissible to shorten the prayer (Salat) while traveling?
questions.answer: Yes, there is no blame on you for shortening the prayer if you fear that those who disbelieve may disrupt you.

questions.text: What is 'Salat al-Khawf' (The Prayer of Fear)?
questions.answer: A specific way of praying in congregation during battle where one group prays while another stands guard, and then they switch.

questions.text: What should believers do for remembrance after completing the prayer?
questions.answer: Remember Allah standing, sitting, or lying on their sides.

questions.text: What is the rule about the timing of the five daily prayers?
questions.answer: Prayer is enjoined on the believers at fixed times.

questions.text: Is secret conversation (Najwa) good according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Most secret conversations are not good, except those that encourage charity, kindness, or reconciliation between people.

questions.text: What happens to someone who opposes the Messenger and follows a path other than that of the believers?
questions.answer: Allah will leave him to what he has turned to and cast him into Hell.

questions.text: How does Shaitan try to deceive people according to this Surah?
questions.answer: He makes false promises and arouses in them sinful desires; his promises are nothing but delusion.

questions.text: What is the only thing that can save a person in the Hereafter, regardless of their gender?
questions.answer: Doing righteous deeds while being a believer; such will enter Paradise and not be wronged even as much as the speck on a date seed.

questions.text: Who is better in religion than one who submits himself to Allah and follows the religion of Ibrahim?
questions.answer: No one; and Allah took Ibrahim as an intimate friend (Khalil).

questions.text: How should believers stand for justice regarding testimony?
questions.answer: They must be persistently standing firm in justice as witnesses for Allah, even if it be against themselves, their parents, or their relatives.

questions.text: Should the wealth or poverty of a person influence a believer's testimony?
questions.answer: No, whether one is rich or poor, Allah is more worthy of both; believers should not follow their desires and deviate from justice.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the hypocrites' prayer?
questions.answer: They stand up to prayer lazily, only to be seen by people, and they remember Allah but little.

questions.text: Where will the hypocrites be placed in the Hereafter?
questions.answer: In the lowest depths of the Fire.

questions.text: Does Allah want to punish people if they are grateful and believe?
questions.answer: No, Allah would not punish you if you are grateful and believe; He is ever Appreciative and Knowing.

questions.text: Does Allah like the public mention of evil speech?
questions.answer: No, He does not like the public mention of evil speech except by one who has been wronged.

questions.text: How does the Surah describe those who believe in some messengers but reject others?
questions.answer: They are the true disbelievers; Allah has prepared for them a humiliating punishment.

questions.text: What did the People of the Book ask the Prophet to do as a sign of his prophethood?
questions.answer: They asked him to bring down to them a book from heaven; they had asked Musa for even greater than that when they said, 'Show us Allah plainly.'

questions.text: What was the consequence for the Children of Israel for breaking their covenant and killing the prophets?
questions.answer: Allah cursed them and sealed their hearts because of their disbelief.

questions.text: What does the Quran say regarding the crucifixion of Prophet Isa (Jesus)?
questions.answer: They did not kill him nor did they crucify him, but it was made to appear so to them; rather, Allah raised him to Himself.

questions.text: Is there any among the People of the Book who will not believe in Isa before his death?
questions.answer: No, every one of them will believe in him before his death, and on the Day of Resurrection, he will be a witness against them.

questions.text: Why were certain good foods made forbidden for the Jews for a time?
questions.answer: Because of their wrongdoing, their hindering many from the way of Allah, and their taking of usury (Riba).

questions.text: To whom did Allah speak directly among the prophets?
questions.answer: Allah spoke to Musa (Moses) directly.

questions.text: What was the purpose of sending all the messengers?
questions.answer: To bring glad tidings and warnings, so that mankind would have no argument against Allah after the coming of the messengers.

questions.text: Who testifies to the truth of what was revealed to the Prophet Muhammad?
questions.answer: Allah Himself testifies to it, and the angels also testify.

questions.text: What is the command given to the People of the Book regarding 'exaggeration' (Ghuluw) in their religion?
questions.answer: They are told not to commit excess in their religion and not to say about Allah except the truth.

questions.text: What is the correct belief about the Messiah, Isa son of Maryam?
questions.answer: He was only a messenger of Allah and His word which He directed to Maryam and a soul [created at a command] from Him.

questions.text: What does the Surah say about the concept of the Trinity?
questions.answer: It commands: 'Do not say, "Three"; desist—it is better for you. Indeed, Allah is but one God.'

questions.text: Would the Messiah ever disdain to be a servant of Allah?
questions.answer: No, neither the Messiah nor the angels near to Allah would ever disdain to be His servants.

questions.text: What is the 'clear light' that Allah has sent down to mankind?
questions.answer: The Quran, which serves as a proof from your Lord.

questions.text: What is the rule of inheritance for 'Kalalah' (a person who dies leaving neither parents nor children)?
questions.answer: If a man dies leaving a sister, she gets half of the estate; if a woman dies leaving a brother, he inherits her entire estate if she has no child.
